rationale: The previous attempt asserted the ensures-foralls as if they were free hypotheses, but they're actually the antecedent of an implication in the ensures, so the asserts failed. Wrap the proof in `if antecedent { ... }` so the foralls become hypotheses inside the branch, then use `assert forall ... implies ... by { ... }` to instantiate both disjoint triggers `post1_self_@[i]` and `post2_self_@[i]` at the same `i`, and close with Seq extensionality `=~=`. After the `if`, Verus has `antecedent ==> post1_self_@ == post2_self_@`, which is exactly what `det_init2zero_equal` needs (the units `r1`, `r2` are trivially equal).
